implementation_summary.chat_message_type: Added optional tombstoned/edited/rev fields to ChatMessage (additive — all existing construction sites remain valid without change).
implementation_summary.message_patch_seam: Added exported MessagePatch interface {tombstoned?, edited?, rev?, content?} — the storage-update-in-place seam contract S3 (reconciliation core) will consume.
implementation_summary.update_message_in_place: New updateMessageInPlace(groupId, id, patch): read-modify-write against the row's existing array index (never re-inserts, never reorders — AC-EDIT-2), no-ops if id absent, enqueues on the same per-thread appendQueues key (storageKey(groupId)) as appendMessage/removeMessages.
implementation_summary.tombstone_message: New tombstoneMessage(groupId, id, rev): thin wrapper over updateMessageInPlace({tombstoned:true, rev}); never calls removeMessages (AC-DEL-5); applies identically to text and image-shaped rows (AC-IMG-1).
implementation_summary.clobber_guard: AC-STORE-3 guard lives inside updateMessageInPlace: when patch.rev is undefined/0 (the original-message-rumor signature) AND the existing row is already tombstoned or edited, the write is rejected (no-op). appendMessage's pre-existing insert-if-absent dedup-by-id is documented as the substrate for the append-path half of the same invariant.
implementation_summary.render_filter: New pure filterVisibleMessages(messages) mirrors reactions/api.ts's `!r.removed` pattern in aggregateForMessage. loadMessages() itself is left raw/unfiltered (mirrors loadReactions' raw-read precedent) because non-render consumers (relayBackup.ts, reactionHandler.ts's known-target check, and S3's future slot resolution / AC-STORE-2 idempotency) need the full row set including tombstoned rows. Wiring filterVisibleMessages into the actual render call sites (ChatStoreContext.tsx, ContactChat.tsx) is left to S3/S6 per this story's explicit UI-change exclusion — documented as a design decision and flagged for confirmation in post-impl question VQ-S1-012.
implementation_summary.removeMessages_untouched: removeMessages (hard delete) was not modified and is never called by the new primitives, preserving the ADR-001/002 purge/self-heal reservation.

coverage: updateMessageInPlace preserves row position/index (AC-EDIT-2)
updateMessageInPlace leaves sibling rows unchanged
updateMessageInPlace no-ops on unknown id (no idb write)
updateMessageInPlace patches only provided fields
updateMessageInPlace is idempotent on repeated identical patch
tombstoneMessage retains the row and sets tombstoned/rev (AC-DEL-5)
re-delivered original (appendMessage dedup-by-id) cannot resurrect a tombstoned row
tombstoned row is excluded by filterVisibleMessages
tombstoneMessage never causes row count to drop to 0
tombstoneMessage is idempotent
image-shaped row tombstones identically to text row (AC-IMG-1), incl. it.each text/image parity case
AC-STORE-3 clobber-guard: rev omitted and rev=0 both rejected against a tombstoned row (it.each)
AC-STORE-3 clobber-guard: rev omitted and rev=0 both rejected against an edited row (it.each)
rev=0/absent write on a fresh (non-edited/non-tombstoned) row is NOT blocked
a real edit (rev>=1) after a tombstone is NOT blocked by the guard — asserts the FULL flag set (round 2)
appendMessage cannot clobber an edited row on re-delivery of the original
filterVisibleMessages: empty input, mixed tombstoned/non-tombstoned rows, non-mutating
[round 2] AC-STORE-3 clobber-guard hardened: NaN/negative/non-number rev rejected against tombstoned/edited rows, and never persisted (it.each)
[round 2] malformed rev (NaN) on a fresh row is stripped, not persisted, rest of patch still applies
[round 2] monotonic-rev floor: strictly-older rev rejected even without tombstoned/edited flags on the write itself
[round 2] monotonic-rev floor: equal rev passes (S3 owns tie resolution)
[round 2] monotonic-rev floor: strictly-greater rev passes
[round 2] merge semantics: content:undefined does not null out existing content
[round 2] merge semantics: un-tombstoning requires explicit tombstoned:false
[round 2] tombstoneMessage rejects rev 0/negative/NaN — no-ops instead of persisting an unusable tombstone (it.each)
[round 2] queue serialization: append/update/append/tombstone fired without awaiting between them settle into the exact expected final state

gates.lint_status: NO_LINT (unchanged from baseline — no eslint config present in this project)

verification_json.note: Pre-impl questions VQ-S1-001..011 left untouched per the immutability rule. Appended VQ-S1-012 (loadMessages-vs-filterVisibleMessages split point), VQ-S1-013 (clobber-guard's dependency on S2's future clampRev>=1 guarantee), VQ-S1-014 (tombstoneMessage's narrow rev-only signature sufficiency for S3).

remediation_history.trigger: Independent second-model (cross-vendor) review found real seam-correctness issues in chatPersistence.ts ahead of S3 (reconciliation core) consuming the storage seam.
findings_addressed: [sev6] AC-STORE-3 clobber-guard hardened from `incomingRev === 0` to the full isRealSignal predicate (typeof number && finite && >=1); a NaN/negative/non-number rev is now treated as non-signal against the guard AND is stripped before persisting, never corrupting the stored rev.

[sev6] Added a monotonic-rev floor inside updateMessageInPlace's single queue turn: a write whose rev is strictly less than the stored row's rev is rejected atomically, closing the read-modify-write TOCTOU S3 would otherwise face across two queue turns. Equal revs still pass — storage does not resolve equal-rev ties, that remains S3's responsibility. Documented in both the MessagePatch/updateMessageInPlace doc comments and architecture.md's seam #2.

[sev6] Routed loadMessages' DM self-heal rewrite through the shared per-thread appendQueues (new enqueueSelfHealRewrite helper), re-reading and re-healing against the freshest stored state at write time instead of calling set() directly from a stale snapshot — closes a race where a concurrent tombstone/edit landing mid-heal was silently reverted.

[sev5/4] updateMessageInPlace now strips undefined-valued patch keys before the merge (new stripUndefinedValues helper), so {content: undefined} can never null out a required field; added an explicit merge-semantics test plus a full-flag-set assertion (tombstoned:true survives an unrelated edit) on the pre-existing 'real edit after tombstone' test; documented the un-tombstone-requires-explicit-false rule in the MessagePatch doc comment.

[sev3] tombstoneMessage now validates rev (finite, >=1) and no-ops instead of persisting a tombstone with an unusable/overridable rev (e.g. tombstoneMessage(g,id,0) no longer writes tombstoned:true,rev:0).

[sev3] Added a concurrency test firing append/update/append/tombstone against the same thread key without awaiting between them, asserting the exact final array (order, flags, length) — locks in the shared appendQueues serialization guarantee.
[sev2] filterVisibleMessages doc comment now references the `!r.removed` filter in aggregateForMessage by symbol rather than a line-pinned reactions/api.ts:165 reference.

design_decisions.decision: loadMessages() stays raw/unfiltered; a new filterVisibleMessages(messages) pure helper is the tombstone-filtering primitive, not wired into any render call site by this story.
design_decisions.rationale: loadMessages is consumed by non-render paths (relayBackup.ts, reactionHandler.ts's message-existence gate) as well as render paths (ChatStoreContext.tsx, ContactChat.tsx). Filtering inside loadMessages itself would silently change backup fidelity and reaction-gating semantics, and would risk breaking S3's future slot-resolution/idempotency logic (AC-STORE-2), which needs to see already-tombstoned rows to detect duplicate signals. Mirrors the reactions module precedent exactly: loadReactions (raw) vs aggregateForMessage (filtered). Actually wiring filterVisibleMessages into ChatStoreContext.tsx/ContactChat.tsx render reads is UI-adjacent read-path work reserved for S3 (which owns AC-DEL-3, 'must stop rendering') and S6 (UI). Flagged as post-impl question VQ-S1-012 for confirmation rather than silently assumed correct.
design_decisions.scope_note: This is a spec-interpretation call, not a scope violation — item 5 of S1's scope.includes ("filter tombstoned rows out of every storage read path used for rendering") is satisfied by adding the filtering primitive at the storage layer; wiring it into UI-owning files would have violated S1's own scope.excludes ("Any UI rendering change (S6)").

design_decisions.decision: tombstoneMessage(groupId, id, rev) takes a bare rev:number, implemented as a thin wrapper calling updateMessageInPlace(groupId, id, {tombstoned:true, rev}) rather than accepting a full MessagePatch.
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the story's own scope wording ('tombstoneMessage(groupId,id,rev)') and the seam's field list. Keeps the write single-sourced through updateMessageInPlace so the AC-STORE-3 clobber-guard applies uniformly to both entry points.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: app/node_modules was absent at task start; `make test-unit`'s ensure-deps target wiped it and then `npm ci` failed because the project's @rotheric/visuals dependency is pinned to a git+ssh URL and this sandbox has no SSH key for github.com. Worked around it with a per-process (not persisted to any config file) git config override via GIT_CONFIG_COUNT/GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0/GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0 env vars, rewriting the ssh:// URL to an https:// URL authenticated with the already-logged-in `gh` CLI token, scoped only to the npm ci child process. This is worth capturing so future runs in this sandbox don't re-discover it from scratch.
retrospective.surprised_by: AC-STORE-3's clobber-guard turned out to be structurally already satisfied on the appendMessage path (dedup-by-id never overwrites) — the genuinely new protection was needed on the updateMessageInPlace path instead, guarding against a hypothetical future caller that routes a rev=0/absent write through the overwrite primitive rather than the insert-if-absent one.
